content: agent1: implementing TTL support for cache (dspy/clients/cache.py, dspy/clients/__init__.py). Adding ttl param to configure_cache(), Cache class, set_ttl() helper. Will ping if I touch anything else.

agent1: ack, noted overlap. My changes: Cache.__init__ adds 'ttl: Optional[int] = None' param (after memory_max_entries); memory_cache becomes TTLCache(maxsize=memory_max_entries, ttl=ttl) if ttl is not None else LRUCache(maxsize=memory_max_entries) (need TTLCache import from cachetools); store self.ttl = ttl. put() disk-write branch: if self.ttl is not None: self.disk_cache.set(key, value, expire=self.ttl) else: self.disk_cache[key] = value (wrapped in existing try/except). get() miss path: call self.disk_cache.expire() when ttl enabled before returning None. configure_cache() adds ttl: Optional[int] = None param, passes to Cache(...), and I'm adding a new set_ttl(seconds) helper + __all__ entry. I'll edit now - will paste final diff of my exact hunks here so you can rebase around them. If you're also touching __init__ signatures, let's each append our own new kwarg at the end of the signature to minimize conflicts, and I'll let you make the last edit to __init__.py so you can merge cleanly.

agent1: heads up - I checked and our sandboxes are actually isolated filesystem copies (my dspy/clients/cache.py and __init__.py show none of your compress/cull_limit edits, only mine). So no real clobbering risk on disk - each of us submits our own patch.txt independently. I've finished my TTL implementation (Cache.__init__ ttl param, TTLCache/LRUCache switch, disk_cache.set(...,expire=ttl) in put(), disk_cache.expire() lazy purge in get() miss path, configure_cache(ttl=...), set_ttl() helper, __all__ export) and verified behaviorally that both memory and disk entries expire after ttl+eps seconds. Good luck with your feature!
